items.title: Iris species from petal measurements
items.scenario: A botany app has 150 labeled iris flowers with sepal/petal length and width. Users photograph a flower and enter four measurements; the app suggests species (setosa, versicolor, virginica).
items.explanation: Classic 3-class k-NN: standardize the four features, pick k via cross-validation (often k = 5–11), majority vote among k nearest training flowers. Boundaries are nonlinear in raw measurement space without explicit feature engineering.

items.title: Movie recommendation by user taste vector
items.scenario: A streaming service represents each user as a vector of genre ratings (action, comedy, drama, …). For a new user with partial ratings, predict whether they will like a unseen title from similar users' ratings.
items.explanation: k-NN in user space: find k users with closest rating vectors (cosine or Euclidean after scaling), aggregate their scores on the target movie. Regression variant averages star ratings; classification variant thresholds like/dislike.

items.title: House price from nearby sales
items.scenario: A realtor tool has geocoded past sales with sq ft, beds, and sale price. For a new listing, estimate price from the k most similar sold homes in the same market.
items.explanation: k-NN regression: neighbors selected by distance on (lat, lon, sq ft, beds) after scaling; ŷ = mean price of k neighbors. Local market shifts make k and feature weights sensitive — validation RMSE on held-out sales is essential.

items.title: Handwritten digit with 1-NN baseline
items.scenario: MNIST-style 784-pixel images flattened to vectors. A team runs 1-NN on a 10k training subset as a sanity-check baseline before training a CNN.
items.explanation: 1-NN with Euclidean distance on raw pixels is weak but instructive: high d, unscaled pixel brightness dominates. Same pipeline with PCA-reduced features or proper scaling shows how preprocessing changes k-NN performance.

items.title: Fraud alert from transaction feature similarity
items.scenario: A bank stores historical transactions labeled fraud/normal with amount, merchant category, time-of-day, and velocity features. A new transaction triggers a lookup for k nearest past transactions.
items.explanation: k-NN classification flags risk if majority of k neighbors are fraud. Feature scaling is critical (amount in dollars vs hour 0–23); imbalanced labels may need weighted vote or larger k — evaluation uses precision/recall, not accuracy alone.
